mnemonics: Radio Waves Travel Far But Microwaves & Infrared Need Line-of-Sight
keyPoints: Unguided media transmits data without a physical conductor, using electromagnetic waves.
The three main types are radio waves, microwaves, and infrared waves.

Each type has distinct characteristics: propagation method (omnidirectional vs. directional), frequency range, and common applications (e.g., radio for long-range, Bluetooth; microwaves for satellite; infrared for short-range, remote controls).
commonMistakes: Believing unguided media is less secure than guided media; it is often more vulnerable to eavesdropping.
Confusing the propagation methods, e.g., thinking microwaves are omnidirectional like radio waves when they are highly directional and require line-of-sight.
